mechanism_narrative: A declining FEDFUNDS rate lowers the discount rate applied to equities and improves financial conditions, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next month when the policy rate is still falling from restrictive levels.
failure_condition: Reject if 20-day forward SPX returns are not positive on average during months with a month-over-month decline in FEDFUNDS, or if the signal's hit rate falls below 50% over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A CPIAUCSL inflation acceleration above the prior month raises expected policy tightening and compresses equity multiples, so S&P 500 forward returns turn negative over the next 20 trading days when inflation momentum crosses higher.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL rises above its 6-month trend and FEDFUNDS is below CPIAUCSL inflation momentum
failure_condition: Reject if triggered episodes do not produce negative 20-day SPX returns in at least 60% of cases, or if inflation surprises fail to coincide with weaker equity performance over any 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: A rising UNRATE signals labor-market deterioration, weaker household income growth, and lower earnings expectations, which should depress S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Reject if month-over-month UNRATE increases are followed by positive 20-day SPX returns more often than not, or if the average forward return after unemployment rises is not below the unconditional mean.

mechanism_narrative: A synchronized decline in PAYEMS and rise in UNRATE marks a labor-market break that weakens expected sales and profits, causing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS falls for 2 consecutive months and UNRATE rises by at least 0.2 percentage points over the same span
failure_condition: Reject if the joint trigger does not precede negative 60-day SPX returns in at least 60% of occurrences, or if either labor variable alone explains the signal equally well.

mechanism_narrative: Rising RSAFS indicates stronger consumer spending and revenue momentum, which should lift earnings expectations and support positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Reject if upward RSAFS momentum is not followed by above-average 20-day SPX returns, or if the sign of the return relationship turns negative for any sustained 2-year period.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp drop in HOUST below its recent trend signals housing demand contraction and a wider demand slowdown, which should lead to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: HOUST falls more than 10% below its 6-month average while GDPC1 growth is decelerating
failure_condition: Reject if housing weakness occurs without weaker 20-day SPX returns in a majority of triggered cases, or if GDP deceleration adds no incremental explanatory power.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained rise in INDPRO reflects stronger industrial output and capacity utilization, supporting cyclical earnings and positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Reject if upward INDPRO trends fail to predict positive 60-day SPX returns, or if the average return after positive industrial momentum is indistinguishable from zero over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When INDPRO weakens while CPIAUCSL remains elevated, the market reads the combination as stagflation risk: margin pressure rises and real activity slows, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO declines month over month while CPIAUCSL inflation stays above its 12-month average
failure_condition: Reject if the combined weak-output/high-inflation condition does not outperform either series alone in predicting negative 20-day SPX returns, or if triggered episodes produce positive returns in more than 40% of cases.
